essence: Studies air pollution, analyses atmospheric data, and helps protect public health and the environment.

identity.title: Air Quality Scientist
identity.summary: Monitors air pollutants, analyses atmospheric samples, models air‑quality trends, and supports environmental regulation and public‑health protection.

salary.notes: Scientists specialising in atmospheric modelling, industrial emissions, or regulatory investigations can exceed £75k-£95k+.

qualifications: Training in environmental science, atmospheric science, chemistry, or physics
Knowledge of air‑quality standards and pollution sources
Ability to conduct atmospheric sampling and laboratory analysis
Understanding of dispersion modelling, emissions, and public‑health impacts

lifestyle.description: Air quality scientists work for environmental agencies, councils, research organisations, and consultancies. They monitor pollutants, analyse atmospheric data, investigate air‑quality incidents, and support environmental regulation. The role suits individuals who enjoy science, modelling, and environmental protection.

habits: Models pollution dispersion and air‑quality trends
Investigates pollution incidents and emissions sources
